methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing

data.narrative:
QuantumDiamonds is a seed-stage quantum computing company founded by Kevin Berghoff and Fleming Bruckmaier. The broader quantum computing sector includes 3 public comparables (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private peers, including later-stage companies such as IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Traditional peer-median valuation is not applicable here. The quantum computing sector lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables at seed to support a reliable multiple-based analysis; only 7 peers exist in quantum computing overall, and none satisfy narrow stage or valuation-tier filters. A cross-sector seed median would introduce distortion given quantum's capital intensity and technical differentiation. The public comps (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) span a range of business models and maturity levels that do not cleanly proxy for a seed-stage hardware or software player in the space.

This analysis cannot be completed with the data constraints present. QuantumDiamonds has raised €7M in seed capital plus a €152M facility commitment, which anchors minimum valuation context but does not itself justify a post-money valuation absent asset, revenue, or IP benchmarking against peers. Valuation would require bottom-up analysis tied to company-specific technical milestones, IP position, or pathway to Series A, rather than peer triangulation. Recommend revisiting once comparable seed or early-stage quantum companies with disclosed valuations enter the dataset.
